So now that you have a foundation of the straight line system from the ebook, I want to take you inside a live event. So you actually see me going over this stuff, you know, live, right on video. And let me just set this clip up for you here. It really starts, and if you've seen the movie, you probably know some of this, where on my first day on Wall Street where I became a stockbroker, it was actually Black Monday, but the market went down 508 points in a single day. Just like that, after watching all these other brokers get rich, and I was their slave just you know, smiling and dialing, right, and passing the phone to another broker, my turn came and bam, the firm was out of business. That night when I came home and I, you know, walked through that door and my wife, she had no idea, there was a wife number one back then, and no idea that the market had even crashed. She wasn't much of a news bug. And that was really when I came, when I collapsed in her arms and I took that first punch that we all take as entrepreneurs and also salespeople, and I took that punch and I was literally paralyzed with fear and uncertainty. All my hopes and dreams had been dashed with the market crashing and I was out of a job. And at first, you know, I was going to leave Wall Street altogether and I started looking through the help wanted section of the paper and I stumbled upon an ad for a brokerage firm in Long Island. And that, and you remember I'm sure from the movie, it's a classic scene when I walk into this firm and these guys are sitting around, they're all disheveled, you know, selling going to the bathroom, the toilet flushes, right? And I walk in there and they're selling penny stocks. And again, I didn't know what a penny stock was at the time. And when I heard these guys selling on the phone, they were just lying, they were just terrible salespeople. They, most of them couldn't even sell the weight of a paper bag basically, right? They were just awful salespeople. And I sat down and I realized the commissions were huge and they, you know, handed me a list of seven stocks. So I picked one which I think it was the dog with the least fleas, it was called Arncliffe National. And I started writing myself a script. And again, this is pretty straight line, just my own ideas of what I should be saying on the phone to close the deal. And I heard a lot of stuff those seven or eight months when I was training on Wall Street, right? Now it was my chance to shine on the telephone. You know, I wrote the script, that it was when it was done, it was really, really good. And I picked up the phone, the first person I called, the words just started coming out perfectly. I had this knack for selling on the telephone. And as I, and this is a classic scene again from the movie where they're watching me and they, you know, they can't believe what they're hearing because I sound so good on the phone. And I did. And I ended up closing the guy for, you know, four or $5,000 of this Arncliffe National. And when I'm done, the guy's like, you know, how'd you do that? What were you doing? And from that point forward, I developed this little sort of cult following of these, you know, young guys that would listen to me. And I was training them. It was pretty straight line. We were selling penny stocks to average moms and pops. It was a pretty easy sale as far as, you know, my book, right? And then ultimately I opened up my own firm. And then when I made that transition, I spoke about before and started selling to the richest 1% of Americans, those $5 stocks, that was when the system broke down. This is before the straight line. Very often with systems, you don't really know how good they are until you stress it at the highest level. And that's when the cracks, the fissures start to appear. And when I went to this sort of high level selling, when, you know, you're calling rich people, it was sophisticated, you know, they, you know, they're owners, people of power. And these young guys are trying to call them with some sort of, you know, very disorganized system I had back then before the straight line. It broke down. And that was really what forced me to invent this unbelievably effective way of taking any human being and show them how they can become a world-class closer by essentially taking a step back before they start to sell and realizing some very fundamental things about any situation of influence, any negotiation. You know, having to do with selling or you try to get someone to do something or believe something or buy into an idea that you have. And it starts with this understanding that there are certain things that need to line up in every sale. And those things are always the same. And by putting them in a straight line, a linear fashion, bam, what happens is truly staggering in terms of you being able to close the deal. So again, let's go to the live tape now here. And I want you to enjoy it, listen very, very closely, get your pen and paper, write things down and I'll see you on the other side. So here's what I did. And I will explain to you exactly what it's all about and give you the basics of the straight line. I can't give you all of it now, not because I don't want to, because we'll have to go to seven or eight o'clock at night. But let me give you as much as I can, okay, and show you how you can apply this to your business without even knowing it. Just a little bit of it, two percent of it will allow you to start becoming more effective at persuading people. So here's the deal. I went that moment when I realized I had to retrain everybody. I said, everyone put down, we cursed a lot. I said, put down the fucking phones. Put down the fucking phones, everybody. Put down their phones. All right. I want you to take every piece of paper on your desk and throw it in the fucking garbage. Rip it up. We start from scratch. Everything you've learned, everything you know to this point. I want you to forget everything you're about to relearn. Everything from the time you were this big about language communication and something happened to me. And I popped into a state of such certainty where I was channeling. I believed that I was really channeling stuff from somewhere else because I started having access to knowledge. And they told me, who's the thinking grow rich? Anybody here? Which is, if you haven't read it, read it. It's by Napoleon Hill. It's a fabulous book. It's one of the first, I think it's the first book I read on wealth and success changed my life in many, many ways. Okay. An amazing book. And they talk about this, the universal force. And it sounds kind of kooky, you know, come on, it's all bullshit, right? But something happens in some moments in your life and whatever it is you're able to access sort of this higher intelligence within yourself and channel. And I, words started coming out of my own mouth and I didn't even know what was going on. And I devised this system. What I said to them was basically this. I said, the days of this, you know, of going out and carpet bombing and calling people with no money and trying to get into an investment they don't have, it's over, we're done from now, we only speak to the wealthiest people in the world. I said, and there's three things you have to establish within four seconds of the conversation, the moment you pick up that phone. And let me taste thing. When, who here would like to learn to attract more affiliates in their life? Raise your hand. Okay. Who would like to have better relationships with their, um, critical processes? I said, there's three things that have to be established. The moment you pick up that phone, you have four seconds. If you don't establish this in the first four seconds of a conversation, you're done. Now you have about 10 more seconds in truth of catch up all, but if you're at 14 seconds, you don't have to establish, you can't influence anybody. You're done. And here's what those four things are. Number one, in four seconds, they must know that you are number one, sharp as attack. Two, enthusiastic as hell. And three, an expert in your field. If those three things are not established, like you're done. Why is that? Well, in truth, okay, think about this, you know, do you really want to deal with a freaking novice when you go and buy a car or you buy a stock, you do anything? Who do you want to get a haircut? Who do you want to deal with the pro or a novice pro? Of course, right? Okay. Let me tell you something. Okay. As human beings, it's just we judge books by their covers. Our moms told us not to, our dads told us not to. We make instant decisions about people. The second we see them, we size them up. And because nowadays most of communication occurs on the phone over our cell phones, you're not even seeing them. So how is that person sizing you up if they can't see you? You're in a vobingo, your tonality. There are two things you establish with the tonality and body language when you're in person. And those two together make up approximately 90 or 91% of human communication. And you've probably heard that or read that somewhere else. It's a fact. It's true. 91% is body language, tonality. 9% is what? The words. Yet when I walk into any phone room or hear any person, if I heard one of you on the phone trying to influence an affiliate, a credit card processor, a customer, I promise you that maybe only this many of you, very sliver, would actually be maximizing their tonality to establish these three things within four. And then you have another 10 seconds to play catch up ball or you're done. You're not doing it. And it's so easy to do. There are overall, there are 29 tonal patterns. You're like, fuck, 29. That's too much for them. You're already using them. You just don't know it. And because you don't know what they are and what they can do emotionally to the person you're speaking to, you don't trigger them at the right time. You understand that concept? You do it. You're saying this stuff. Who's had a time in their life where they felt really certain and spoke certain? Raise your hand. Okay, fine. Who here has had a time in their life when they felt like something was really in short supply and they called someone and told the secret? Raise your hand. Who's told the secret before? Who hasn't told the secret in their life? Come on. Everyone, raise your hand if you've told the secret. When you tell a secret, what do you do? You whisper. Okay? Let me tell you something. Okay? Whispering on a phone call influencing is so powerful when you whisper at the right moment, when you lower your voice, when you raise your voice, when you lower your voice. Speaking up, slowing down. Taking a declarative statement and turning it into a question. You must become the most effective communicator you can be and that means mastering the art of tonality. Using your voice, the rhythm, the tones to imply hidden meanings to the words that you say. What do you think happens when something's italicized in writing and you stress it? Or there's a question mark where it shouldn't be in a sales letter and something is bold and something is in light type. It's tonality on paper. I'm an author and I, it took me, I found it very difficult to become an author. Writing did not come easy to me. It took me a long time to really become a competent writer because I didn't realize the power of tonality through grammar and like, it's a different art. But once you master the art of tonality, influencing becomes really, really easy because the words the language patterns are there. I can hand them to you on a platter. The language patterns to influence are there. Some of them border on hypnotic language patterns. Some of them are very basic and just cut to logical ways the brain works. Some cut to emotional reasons why people take action. But the language patterns are easy. What people don't pay enough attention to is what really influences people, which is tonality. And secondly, your body language. The second and here, and here's the irony of body language. When it's in person, people start sizing up at 1.24 of a second. One they just, they just study what is meant to this is a brain machine where they can actually see what's happening as you see a new person and they saw it boom, 1.24 of a second, up and down, huge spike and you're down. The brain calculates that fast in body language. So when I teach people how to become effective influencers and closers, it's not about becoming some hard charging, you know, cliched sales person. Sometimes that's called for in some industries, but more often it's not about that. More often it's about being mellow and being persuasive by using tonalities that are pleasing to the ear and that inserts subliminal messages as you're talking. And here's what those messages are. Number one, A, that you care. I care. This is the number one most important thing that has to be established on a subconscious, not a conscious, tell someone I really care about you. They'll say, what a fricking lion sack of shit salesman this is. I care, I really care about you. People don't believe you when you say you care. Ultimately, when they know you, yes, you get to that point. The way you establish that you care about someone is through your tone of voice, from an empathetic, sympathetic tone used in precisely the right point of an oral presentation. You can, and so on, they won't even know why the people will feel that, wow, this guy really cares, this girl, she really cares about me. So number one is the other established that you care. Number two, that you're just like them. People associate, people make decisions, want to do business with people who are like them, not who are opposite of them. When you go out and join a club, a country club or any organization, you don't say, wow, this is a really great place. This is not a sole there who's like me. No. You go and say, wow, everyone else is a cool place. They have shared my interests, they're just like me, so you join, you associate. When you're trying to influence somebody, okay, the perception has to be, I'm just like you. Now, the mistake most salespeople make, okay, and what I'm getting at here is something called, starts with R, anyone want to guess it? Rapport, right, which is where we all know well, which is the most misunderstood word in the English language in my book, it's ridiculous. What people think constitutes rapport is often repulsive. You know what repulsive means? To repel. I don't mean like you're repulsive looking, okay, but you can either be repulsive or attractive. You know, who knows repulsive people? Raise your hand. We all know repulsive people. What is it about them that's repulsive? Usually their words, more or not, it's their body language and their fricking tone of, you can't even talk to them. And other people, yet when they talk to you, they have what's called charisma. You just want to be attracted to them, you want to be around them, you want to do business with them. Some people say, well, you know, Jordan, and here's a reason. People would be like, I don't have charisma, it's not who I am. Shut up. Get some fucking charisma. You can learn. It's just this, that's bullshit. That's the bullshit story. You, anybody, can learn to have charisma. Because it's a bit of body language and tonality. That's it. And not saying stupid shit, okay? But mostly it's about body language. Don't tell me you don't have charisma. I'll teach you to have charisma. It's not some magic fricking bottle, okay? The point is, is this sort of that magic bullet, you know, that's charisma? It's sort of like, you know, what is it? It's fucking simple, everybody. Tonality, body language, without saying stupid shit. So what I taught my brokers, okay, and I really didn't even know this. It's like now I have it broken down so mathematically, because what happened many years later is when I, when the book came out and the movie was announced, I started getting offers to come speak all over the world. And I was like shit, well, they were like, train our salesmen, train us to come closers all over. Like Deutsche Bank, Goldman Sachs, Credit Suisse, you know, our world, our biggest brokerage from the world, companies like Symantec, huge companies, I'm like fuck, well they want me to, like, we need three hours to teach, I'm like three hours, I had these guys captive for years. See what happened was, so I started teaching these guys the straight line system. And I'll show you visually what the straight line is. I called it the straight line because out of sheer frustration, like every morning I'd give a meeting for about an hour, and every afternoon I'd give another meeting for about an hour and a half, every day for five years. As the firm started with 12 people and grew to thousands. And imagine a room about twice the size of this, packed from front to back with desks, okay, people banging away on the phone, pacing around, it was absolutely insane. You walk in here like holy shit, is this an alternative universe? That's what happened ultimately. But in the beginning it was 12 guys, okay, and trying to explain to them, like, don't, I was like, don't you fucking, I was like, I was killing me inside, they were engaged. I said, every sale is the fucking same. They're like, what? Like every sale is the same, every encounter, every negotiation, every sale is the same. I'm like, watch, it's a fucking straight line. Here's your open here, and I was like, that's how fresh I was like, here's the open, here's the closed, right? Now there's going to be, every sale they'll try to go off the line, right? Here and here, below and above, right? These are the boundaries. You will not allow the encounter to go outside the boundaries. And that is called what? Controlling the sale at the essence of effective communication, at the essence of influence in your business right now. Once you learn to control the linguistic encounter, you will be shocked at what will happen. So I taught them, number one, to control the sale. Here's the perfect sale. They agree with everything you say, from the first word you utter, they're on board with you. They're saying yes to every question you ask. Perfect sale. How often does that happen? Not too often, right? So what happens is, is the customer has a tendency, the prospect, to go off the line. They want to go off the line, you want to keep them on the line. What you have here between here and here is your healthy boundaries of where the sale can go. As long as the sale stays within these boundaries, you're in control. If it goes outside the boundaries, you're out of control. In the boundaries, there's two things that you're doing. Number one, you're establishing instant massive rapport through tonality and body language. And number two, using that rapport to gather intelligence. To find out what the prospect truly needs, what his true desires are. Because here's the deal. Remember I said those three things, what are they? Sharp is attack, or sharp is attack, enthusiastic, and what else? Expert. Expert, you feel those three things, right? But why? What is the higher purposes? What it really means is this. What this all rolls up to, one thing is that you are a person worth listening to. And taking it one step higher than that, because you're someone who could help them achieve their goals. You could help them get what they want. That's why these three things are so important, because they chunk up to a higher purpose, which is that you're a guy that can help them, or a girl who can help them get what they want in life. If they don't think you can help them, they're not going to want to talk to you. You can help them get what they want.
